L1.area: Workload autoscaling and resource management controllers

L2.description: A nil pointer dereference vulnerability exists in the controller manager. When a HorizontalPodAutoscaler object is created or modified through the v1 API with partially specified scaling behavior configuration (e.g., specifying scale-down behavior but omitting scale-up behavior), the controller does not validate that all required behavior fields are populated before accessing them. This results in a nil pointer dereference that causes the controller manager to panic and enter a crash loop, leading to denial of service.

L3.area: Workload autoscaling and resource management controllers
L3.description: A nil pointer dereference vulnerability exists in the controller manager. When a HorizontalPodAutoscaler object is created or modified through the v1 API with partially specified scaling behavior configuration (e.g., specifying scale-down behavior but omitting scale-up behavior), the controller does not validate that all required behavior fields are populated before accessing them. This results in a nil pointer dereference that causes the controller manager to panic and enter a crash loop, leading to denial of service.

ground_truth.reason: The HPA controller dereferences ScaleUp and ScaleDown behavior pointers without nil checks, causing a panic when a v1 HPA is created with partially specified behaviors that leave these fields nil after API version conversion.
